title: repeat_alpha
subject:
Write a program that takes one string and prints it, repeating each alphabetical character as many times as its position in the alphabet (a/A = 1, b/B = 2, ... z/Z = 26). Case is preserved. Non-alphabetical characters are printed once. End with a newline.

If the number of arguments is not exactly 1, print just a newline.

Examples:
  $ ./a.out "abc"
  abbccc
  $ ./a.out "Aa"
  Aa
  $ ./a.out "d!"
  dddd!
